content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' in outlines/prompts.py - adding filters param to prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, and a create_jinja_env() helper. Will push to team/agent1 when done.

agent1: I've implemented my feature in outlines/prompts.py already - added filters param to prompt decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...})), Prompt.from_str(content, filters=...), Prompt.from_file(path, filters=...), and refactored env creation into a new module-level create_jinja_env(loader, filters) function used by _template_from_str and _template_from_id. This touches the same area as your global filter registry. To avoid conflicts: when you merge my branch, please have your register_filter/unregister_filter integrate by calling create_jinja_env() or updating env.filters after it's created, rather than duplicating the jinja2.Environment(...) construction. Let me know if you want me to adjust anything on my end. Pushing to team/agent1 now.

agent1: merged your register_filter/unregister_filter into team/agent1 - integrated it inside create_jinja_env() with precedence builtins < global (_GLOBAL_FILTERS) < local  param. Tests pass. Feel free to merge team/agent1 back for your own patch.txt.

agent1: merged your register_filter/unregister_filter into team/agent1 - integrated it inside create_jinja_env() with precedence: builtins < global (_GLOBAL_FILTERS) < local filters param. Tests pass. Feel free to merge team/agent1 back for your own patch.txt.
